title: Panakkattodil Devi Temple
tagline: Panakkattodil Devi Temple — spiritual town of Kerala

overview.short: Panakkattodil Devi Temple (Malayalam: പനയ്ക്കറ്റോടില്‍ ദേവീ ക്ഷേത്രം) is a Hindu temple in Chavara Thekkumbhagom village in Kollam district of the Indian state of Kerala.
overview.description: Panakkattodil Devi Temple (Malayalam: പനയ്ക്കറ്റോടില്‍ ദേവീ ക്ഷേത്രം) is a Hindu temple in Chavara Thekkumbhagom village in Kollam district of the Indian state of Kerala. Primary deity of this temple is Durga. The temple is managed by the Travancore Devaswom Board, Kerala. The Thalappoli in 'Meda Bharani' and the annual festival during the months of April and May are popular.

Fly to nearest airport + road transfer

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Chavara Thekkumbhagom
topPlaces.description: Chavara Thekkumbhagom or Chavara South is an Island village in Kollam District of the state of kerala, India. It is located 10 km from the Kollam City.

topPlaces.name: Ashtamudi Lake
topPlaces.description: Ashtamudi Lake (Ashtamudi Kayal, ml: "അഷ്ടമുടിക്കായൽ" ), in the Kollam District of the Indian state of Kerala is a unique wetland ecosystem and a large palm-shaped (also described as octopus-shaped) water body. It is second only in size to the Vembanad estuary ecosystem of the state.

topPlaces.name: Kottukadu
topPlaces.description: Kottukadu is a small town situated in the Chavara panchayath. Kottukadu Muslim jamaathu is the second most populated jamaath in Kollam district, village is part of the Karunagappally taluk and Chavara Assembly constituency.

topPlaces.name: Kottankulangara Devi Temple
topPlaces.description: The Kottankulangara Devi Temple is a Hindu temple to the goddess Durga Bhagavathy or Aadi Shakthi, the supreme mother of power, located in the village of Chavara, Kerala, India.

topPlaces.name: Prakkulam
topPlaces.description: Prakkulam is a village in Thrikkaruva Grama Panchayat, Kollam district in the south west Indian state of Kerala, 12 km from Kollam junction railway station.

topPlaces.name: Neendakara
topPlaces.description: Neendakara is a suburb of Kollam city in Kerala, India. The twin harbours, Neendakara and Shakthikulangara, are located here.

topPlaces.name: Neendakara Bridge
topPlaces.description: The Neendakara Bridge, located on National Highway 66 in Kollam district, Kerala, India spans 422.5 meters across Ashtamudi Lake. It was inaugurated on February 24, 1972, by T. K. Divakaran, the then Public Works Department Minister.

topPlaces.name: Sambranikodi Island
topPlaces.description: Sambranikodi Island is a landmass situated in the Ashtamudi backwaters of Kollam district, Kerala, India. The island is situated approximately 10 kilometers away from the main city of Kollam.

items.note: Chavara Thekkumbhagom or Chavara South is an Island village in Kollam District of the state of kerala, India. It is located 10 km from the Kollam City.

Ashtamudi Lake (Ashtamudi Kayal, ml: "അഷ്ടമുടിക്കായൽ" ), in the Kollam District of the Indian state of Kerala is a unique wetland ecosystem and a large palm-shaped (also described as octopus-shaped) water body. It is second only in size to the Vembanad estuary ecosystem of the state.

Kottukadu is a small town situated in the Chavara panchayath. Kottukadu Muslim jamaathu is the second most populated jamaath in Kollam district, village is part of the Karunagappally taluk and Chavara Assembly constituency.
The Kottankulangara Devi Temple is a Hindu temple to the goddess Durga Bhagavathy or Aadi Shakthi, the supreme mother of power, located in the village of Chavara, Kerala, India.
Prakkulam is a village in Thrikkaruva Grama Panchayat, Kollam district in the south west Indian state of Kerala, 12 km from Kollam junction railway station.
Neendakara is a suburb of Kollam city in Kerala, India. The twin harbours, Neendakara and Shakthikulangara, are located here.

The Neendakara Bridge, located on National Highway 66 in Kollam district, Kerala, India spans 422.5 meters across Ashtamudi Lake. It was inaugurated on February 24, 1972, by T. K. Divakaran, the then Public Works Department Minister.
Sambranikodi Island is a landmass situated in the Ashtamudi backwaters of Kollam district, Kerala, India. The island is situated approximately 10 kilometers away from the main city of Kollam.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Panakkattodil Devi Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Panakkattodil Devi Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Panakkattodil Devi Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Thiruvananthapuram International Airport (66 km) and the nearest railway station is Thiruvananthapuram Central (68 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Panakkattodil Devi Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Panakkattodil Devi Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Panakkattodil Devi Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Panakkattodil Devi Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Panakkattodil Devi Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Chavara Thekkumbhagom, Ashtamudi Lake, Kottukadu, Kottankulangara Devi Temple, Prakkulam.
